Sunderland.q: What is Sunderland's traditional home kit design?
Sunderland.hint: The club has worn vertical stripes in these two colours for well over a century.

Sunderland.q: In which year did Sunderland win their most recent English league title?
Sunderland.hint: Their sixth championship came in the mid-1930s under Johnny Cochrane.

Sunderland.q: Which side did Sunderland famously beat in the 1973 FA Cup Final?
Sunderland.hint: The Wearsiders shocked the reigning holders and league heavyweights of the day.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland's fierce Tyne–Wear derby is contested against which club?
Sunderland.hint: The rivals sit just up the coast on the Tyne.

Sunderland.q: Whom did Sunderland defeat to lift their first FA Cup in 1937?
Sunderland.hint: Sunderland came from behind to win the Wembley final 3-1 against this Lancashire side.

Sunderland.q: Which Sunderland striker won the European Golden Shoe in 1999–2000?
Sunderland.hint: He top-scored across Europe with 30 Premier League goals that season.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland set a divisional points record in 1998–99 with how many points?
Sunderland.hint: Their First Division tally that season was a record for the division at the time.

Sunderland.q: Who managed Sunderland to the 1998–99 First Division title?
Sunderland.hint: The same manager guided the Phillips–Quinn era into the Premier League.

Sunderland.q: Roy Keane led Sunderland to which title in 2006–07?
Sunderland.hint: In his first managerial season he won promotion back to the top flight.

Sunderland.q: Who is recognised as Sunderland's all-time leading goalscorer?
Sunderland.hint: This 1930s forward scored well over 200 goals and netted in the 1937 Cup Final.

Sunderland.q: Which forward set Sunderland's single-season league scoring record of 43 goals in 1928–29?
Sunderland.hint: His prolific late-1920s haul remains the club's best in one league campaign.

Sunderland.q: In 2009, a Darren Bent goal against Liverpool was deflected past the keeper by what object?
Sunderland.hint: An item thrown from the stands wrong-footed Pepe Reina for one of football's oddest goals.

Sunderland.q: The Stadium of Light was built on the former site of which industry?
Sunderland.hint: A miners' lamp monument outside honours the Wearmouth pit that once stood there.

Sunderland.q: Which Republic of Ireland striker later served as Sunderland's chairman?
Sunderland.hint: The tall forward moved from the pitch to the boardroom after his playing days.

Sunderland.q: When Sunderland won the 1973 FA Cup, which division were they playing in?
Sunderland.hint: Their triumph is celebrated as one of the great cup upsets by a non-top-flight side.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland reached the 2014 League Cup Final, losing to which club?
Sunderland.hint: They led early at Wembley before the eventual Premier League champions hit back to win 3-1.

Sunderland.q: Who managed Sunderland in the 2014 League Cup Final?
Sunderland.hint: The Uruguayan also engineered that season's 'Great Escape' from relegation.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland won the 2021 EFL Trophy, beating which club in the final?
Sunderland.hint: The Wembley final was settled 1-0 against this Merseyside opponent.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland won the 2022 League One play-off final against which side?
Sunderland.hint: A 2-0 Wembley win over the Buckinghamshire club sealed a return to the Championship.

Sunderland.q: In what year was Sunderland AFC founded?
Sunderland.hint: The club began as a schoolteachers' association side in the late 1870s.

Sunderland.q: The first season of 'Sunderland 'Til I Die' documented relegation to which division?
Sunderland.hint: The 2017–18 campaign dropped the club into the third tier.

Sunderland.q: Academy graduate Jordan Pickford was sold in 2017 to which club?
Sunderland.hint: The England goalkeeper joined the Merseyside club for a large fee.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland's record home attendance of 75,118 at Roker Park in 1933 came against which club?
Sunderland.hint: The huge crowd packed in for an FA Cup tie against the East Midlands visitors.

Sunderland.q: Popular goalkeeper Thomas Sørensen represented which country?
Sunderland.hint: The keeper of the Reid era was a full international for his Scandinavian homeland.

Sunderland.q: In their 1973–74 European Cup Winners' Cup run, Sunderland were eliminated by which Portuguese club?
Sunderland.hint: The Lisbon side ended the FA Cup holders' only major European campaign.

Sunderland.q: Which Ghanaian striker became Sunderland's club-record signing in 2010?
Sunderland.hint: The Black Stars forward arrived after a strong 2010 World Cup.

Sunderland.q: Kevin Phillips joined Sunderland in 1997 from which club?
Sunderland.hint: He arrived from the Hertfordshire club for a modest fee before becoming a prolific scorer.

Sunderland.q: Who captained Sunderland's 1937 FA Cup-winning side and was a celebrated creative inside-forward?
Sunderland.hint: The homegrown playmaker also skippered the 1936 title team.

Sunderland.q: Which flamboyant post-war Sunderland forward was nicknamed the 'Clown Prince of Soccer'?
Sunderland.hint: His entertaining style earned him the theatrical moniker in the late 1940s and 50s.

Sunderland.q: Sam Allardyce left Sunderland in 2016 to take which job?
Sunderland.hint: After keeping the club up, he departed for the national team role.

Sunderland.q: David Moyes's 2016–17 season at Sunderland ended in what?
Sunderland.hint: His single full season saw the club drop out of the top flight.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland won the league in 1913 but lost the FA Cup Final that year to which club, narrowly missing the Double?
Sunderland.hint: A single goal from the Midlands side denied them the double in 1913.

Sunderland.q: Who scored the only goal as Sunderland won the 2021 EFL Trophy final?
Sunderland.hint: The USA-born forward struck the winner at Wembley.

Sunderland.q: In which city is the Stadium of Light located?
Sunderland.hint: The ground sits in the North East city the club is named after.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland's record top-flight away win, 9–1 in December 1908, came at which club's ground?
Sunderland.hint: The thrashing was inflicted on their nearest rivals on their own turf.

Sunderland.q: Which French coach was appointed Sunderland head coach in 2024, arriving from Ligue 1's Lorient?
Sunderland.hint: The former Lorient boss took charge for the club's promotion push.

Sunderland.q: Which early-20th-century Sunderland and England forward later became a famous football journalist with his own monthly magazine?
Sunderland.hint: His name lived on in a hugely popular post-war football publication.

Sunderland.q: Sunderland's great side of which decade was dubbed the 'Team of All the Talents'?
Sunderland.hint: The nickname belonged to the dominant championship-winning team of Victorian times.

Sunderland.q: Club great Charlie Hurley played international football for which nation?
Sunderland.hint: Though based in England, the Cork-born defender represented the country of his birth.

Sunderland.q: Which long-serving Sunderland midfielder and captain of the 1990s was nicknamed 'Bally'?
Sunderland.hint: The combative skipper later served the club as a coach and caretaker manager.

Ipswich Town.q: Which manager led Ipswich Town to the 1961-62 First Division title?
Ipswich Town.hint: He would go on to win the 1966 World Cup as England manager.

Ipswich Town.q: Which club did Ipswich beat 1-0 in the 1978 FA Cup final?
Ipswich Town.hint: A single second-half goal settled the final against the Gunners.

Ipswich Town.q: Which Dutch club did Ipswich beat in the 1981 UEFA Cup final?
Ipswich Town.hint: Ipswich prevailed 5-4 on aggregate against the reigning Dutch champions.

Ipswich Town.q: What are Ipswich Town's traditional home colours?
Ipswich Town.hint: The kit is the origin of the club's simple one-word nickname.

Ipswich Town.q: By which nickname are Ipswich Town commonly known?
Ipswich Town.hint: The name nods to the club's rural Suffolk surroundings.

Ipswich Town.q: Which manager led Ipswich to back-to-back promotions in 2023 and 2024?
Ipswich Town.hint: The young Northern Irish coach had arrived from Manchester United's backroom staff.

Ipswich Town.q: Ipswich were promoted to the Premier League in 2024 after finishing where in the Championship?
Ipswich Town.hint: Leicester City finished above them as champions that season.

Ipswich Town.q: Sir Bobby Robson later became manager of which national team?
Ipswich Town.hint: He guided this side to a World Cup semi-final in 1990.

Ipswich Town.q: Who scored the only goal in Ipswich's 1978 FA Cup final win?
Ipswich Town.hint: The midfielder's late strike was his moment of fame, and he was substituted soon after scoring.

Ipswich Town.q: Which Ipswich midfielder scored 14 goals during the 1980-81 UEFA Cup campaign?
Ipswich Town.hint: The Scottish midfielder's tally equalled a European single-season scoring record.

Ipswich Town.q: Who are Ipswich Town's fierce East Anglian rivals?
Ipswich Town.hint: Meetings between the two are billed as the East Anglian derby.

Ipswich Town.q: Alongside Arnold Muhren, which other Dutch international starred in Robson's great Ipswich side?
Ipswich Town.hint: He and Muhren pulled the strings in the UEFA Cup-winning midfield.

Ipswich Town.q: Who holds Ipswich Town's record for most appearances?
Ipswich Town.hint: The full-back racked up over 700 games across roughly two decades.

Ipswich Town.q: Who is Ipswich Town's all-time record goalscorer?
Ipswich Town.hint: This 1960s striker also once scored five in a single European tie.

Ipswich Town.q: In which decade was Ipswich Town founded?
Ipswich Town.hint: The club dates to 1878, long before it turned professional.

Ipswich Town.q: In which season did Ipswich achieve their highest Premier League finish of 5th?
Ipswich Town.hint: George Burley was named Manager of the Year for that campaign.

Ipswich Town.q: Which striker scored five goals in a single game for Ipswich against Southampton in 1982?
Ipswich Town.hint: The Scottish forward's haul came in a 5-2 home win.

Ipswich Town.q: Kevin Beattie was the inaugural winner in 1974 of which award?
o: PFA Young Player of the Year
Ipswich Town.hint: He was the very first player ever to receive it.

Ipswich Town.q: Which family were the long-serving chairmen associated with Ipswich Town through much of the 20th century?
Ipswich Town.hint: This dynasty ran the club through both the Ramsey and Robson eras.

Ipswich Town.q: Which England striker led Ipswich's line in both the 1978 FA Cup and 1981 UEFA Cup triumphs?
Ipswich Town.hint: He was capped by England while at Portman Road.

Ipswich Town.q: Terry Butcher, who came through Ipswich's youth ranks, won international caps for which country?
Ipswich Town.hint: The centre-back became a fixture in the national defence at two World Cups.

Ipswich Town.q: The main stands at Portman Road are named after which two legendary managers?
Ipswich Town.hint: The ground honours its two most successful bosses.

Ipswich Town.q: How did Ipswich Town fare in the 2024-25 Premier League season?
Ipswich Town.hint: They went straight back down after their promotion.

Ipswich Town.q: Who was Ipswich's leading scorer in the 2000-01 Premier League season?
Ipswich Town.hint: The striker's goals were central to that fifth-place finish.

Ipswich Town.q: In which decade did Ipswich Town first join the Football League?
Ipswich Town.hint: They were elected into the Third Division South in 1938.

Ipswich Town.q: Which long-serving Ipswich full-back captained England at the 1982 World Cup?
Ipswich Town.hint: He wore the armband for the national side in Spain.

Ipswich Town.q: Which Ipswich goalkeeper later joined Arsenal and won an England cap around 2000?
Ipswich Town.hint: He was a product of the club's own academy before his move to Highbury.

Ipswich Town.q: Which midfielder was voted PFA Players' Player of the Year in 1981 while at Ipswich?
Ipswich Town.hint: He earned the honour in the same year Ipswich lifted the UEFA Cup.

Ipswich Town.q: What is the approximate capacity of Portman Road?
Ipswich Town.hint: The ground holds a little over thirty thousand spectators.

Ipswich Town.q: Ipswich's 1961-62 league title was remarkable because it came in their first-ever season in which division?
Ipswich Town.hint: They were crowned champions of England in their debut top-flight campaign.

Ipswich Town.q: Which 1970s Ipswich defender, rated by Robson among the finest he coached, was nicknamed 'The Beat'?
Ipswich Town.hint: His surname lent itself naturally to the nickname.

Ipswich Town.q: Which Ipswich striker, famed for a ferociously powerful shot, partnered Ray Crawford under Alf Ramsey?
Ipswich Town.hint: His thunderous shooting complemented Crawford's goals in the title-winning side.

Crystal Palace.q: What is Crystal Palace's nickname?
Crystal Palace.hint: Adopted in 1973 under a manager who redesigned the club's identity.

Crystal Palace.q: In which year was Crystal Palace FC founded?
Crystal Palace.hint: The club took its name from the great glass exhibition hall on the same site.

Crystal Palace.q: Which club did Crystal Palace beat in the 2025 FA Cup Final to win the first major trophy in their history?
Crystal Palace.hint: A 1-0 win at Wembley over one of the dominant sides of the modern era.

Crystal Palace.q: Who scored the winning goal for Crystal Palace in the 2025 FA Cup Final?
Crystal Palace.hint: The England international attacker struck inside the opening 20 minutes.

Crystal Palace.q: Which club did Crystal Palace face in the 1990 FA Cup Final?
Crystal Palace.hint: The tie had to be settled by a replay.

Crystal Palace.q: What was the score in the first match of the 1990 FA Cup Final, after extra time?
Crystal Palace.hint: The sides were level at three apiece before a replay was needed.

Crystal Palace.q: Which substitute scored twice for Palace in the drawn 1990 FA Cup Final?
Crystal Palace.hint: He had suffered a broken leg earlier that season and later starred for Arsenal and England.

Crystal Palace.q: Ian Wright's celebrated strike partner at Crystal Palace in the late 1980s was:
Crystal Palace.hint: The two forwards were spoken of almost as a single attacking pairing.

Crystal Palace.q: Who managed Crystal Palace in the 1990 FA Cup Final?
Crystal Palace.hint: A former Manchester United winger and the club's longest-serving manager.

Crystal Palace.q: Who managed Crystal Palace to victory in the 2025 FA Cup?
Crystal Palace.hint: The Austrian coach appointed early in 2024.

Crystal Palace.q: Who scored Crystal Palace's goal in the 2016 FA Cup Final?
Crystal Palace.hint: His second-half strike gave Palace the lead before they were beaten in extra time.

Crystal Palace.q: Which man scored Palace's extra-time winner in the 1990 FA Cup semi-final and later managed the club in the 2016 final?
Crystal Palace.hint: A midfielder in 1990 who returned to Selhurst Park as boss two decades later.

Crystal Palace.q: Which winger is Crystal Palace's all-time leading goalscorer in the Premier League?
Crystal Palace.hint: Born in the Ivory Coast and raised in south London, he had two spells at the club.

Crystal Palace.q: In 2013 Palace sold Wilfried Zaha to which club, before he returned on loan?
Crystal Palace.hint: Signed by Sir Alex Ferguson in one of his final pieces of transfer business.

Crystal Palace.q: Michael Olise left Crystal Palace in 2024 to join which club?
Crystal Palace.hint: The French winger moved to the reigning German champions.

Crystal Palace.q: Eberechi Eze joined which club in the summer of 2025?
Crystal Palace.hint: He moved to the north London club managed by Mikel Arteta.

Crystal Palace.q: In which year did Selhurst Park first open?
Crystal Palace.hint: Designed by the renowned stadium architect Archibald Leitch.

Crystal Palace.q: Selhurst Park's record attendance of 51,801 was set in 1979 against which club?
Crystal Palace.hint: Set on the night Palace clinched the Second Division title.

Crystal Palace.q: Crystal Palace's highest-ever top-flight finish, third place, came in which season?
Crystal Palace.hint: It came the season after their run to the FA Cup final.

Crystal Palace.q: What are Crystal Palace's traditional home colours?
Crystal Palace.hint: Worn as a distinctive sash, adopted in the early 1970s.

Crystal Palace.q: Which flamboyant manager rebranded Crystal Palace with a new nickname and red-and-blue colours in 1973?
Crystal Palace.hint: A charismatic boss known for his showmanship in the 1970s.

Crystal Palace.q: Terry Venables dubbed his promising late-1970s Palace side the 'Team of the ___'.
Crystal Palace.hint: The young team won promotion to the top flight in 1979.

Crystal Palace.q: Which trophy did Palace win in 1991, beating Everton 4-1 at Wembley?
Crystal Palace.hint: A full members' competition, not counted among the club's major honours.

Crystal Palace.q: Which goalkeeper became Britain's first £1 million goalkeeper when Palace signed him in 1989?
Crystal Palace.hint: He later joined Leeds United and went on to play for England.

Crystal Palace.q: Which long-serving Argentine goalkeeper became a Selhurst Park cult hero across the 2000s and 2010s?
Crystal Palace.hint: He gave the club well over a decade of service and earned a testimonial.

Crystal Palace.q: Which striker scored 21 Premier League goals for Palace in the 2004-05 season?
Crystal Palace.hint: A pacy England forward renowned for winning penalties.

Crystal Palace.q: In 2016 Palace broke their transfer record to sign which striker from Liverpool?
Crystal Palace.hint: A powerful Belgian centre-forward.

Crystal Palace.q: Which Italian winger, formerly of Juventus, joined Palace in 1997 and briefly took charge as player-manager?
Crystal Palace.hint: A bald-headed fan favourite who barely spoke English during his short spell in the dugout.

Crystal Palace.q: Who has been Crystal Palace's chairman since 2010, having led the consortium that rescued the club?
Crystal Palace.hint: He fronted the 'CPFC 2010' takeover out of administration.

Crystal Palace.q: Which manager kept Palace up in 2013-14 and was named the season's best top-flight manager?
Crystal Palace.hint: He took over with the club rock bottom and led a remarkable escape from relegation.

Crystal Palace.q: Which manager coined the word 'bouncebackability' during Palace's 2004 promotion run?
Crystal Palace.hint: His made-up term entered popular football vocabulary.

Crystal Palace.q: Who scored the only goal as Palace won the 2004 play-off final 1-0 against West Ham?
Crystal Palace.hint: He bundled home the winner at Cardiff's Millennium Stadium.

Crystal Palace.q: Who scored the decisive extra-time penalty in Palace's 2013 play-off final win over Watford?
Crystal Palace.hint: A veteran striker converted from the spot at Wembley to seal promotion.

Crystal Palace.q: In their first Premier League season (1992-93), Palace were relegated despite finishing with how many points?
Crystal Palace.hint: They went down on goal difference, a then-record total for a relegated club.

Crystal Palace.q: Crystal Palace are based in which part of London?
Crystal Palace.hint: Their home lies in the borough of Croydon.

Crystal Palace.q: What was Crystal Palace's original nickname before their 1970s rebrand?
Crystal Palace.hint: It referenced the glassworkers of the great exhibition hall.

Crystal Palace.q: Who scored Palace's opening goal in the 1990 FA Cup Final?
Crystal Palace.hint: A defender's header put Palace in front at Wembley.

Crystal Palace.q: At which stadium was the 2025 FA Cup Final played?
Crystal Palace.hint: The national stadium in London.

Crystal Palace.q: Who saved a penalty for Palace in the 2025 FA Cup Final?
Crystal Palace.hint: The England goalkeeper's spot-kick save proved crucial at Wembley.

Crystal Palace.q: Palace's 2025 FA Cup-winning manager had previously won the 2022 Europa League with which club?
Crystal Palace.hint: He led the German side to the trophy on penalties against Rangers.

Crystal Palace.q: In August 2025 Palace won the Community Shield by beating which team on penalties?
Crystal Palace.hint: They edged the reigning Premier League champions in a shoot-out at Wembley.

Crystal Palace.q: Palace's recent captain, centre-back Marc Guehi, plays international football for which country?
Crystal Palace.hint: Though born in Abidjan, he was raised in England and represents them.

Crystal Palace.q: Which club did Palace beat 3-0 in the 2025 FA Cup semi-final?
Crystal Palace.hint: A comfortable Wembley win booked their place in the final.

Crystal Palace.q: Which manager lost his first four league games in charge of Palace in 2017 without the team scoring, and was sacked after 77 days?
Crystal Palace.hint: The Dutchman's spell is among the shortest in Premier League history.

Crystal Palace.q: Which former England manager took charge of Palace in 2017 and returned again in 2023?
Crystal Palace.hint: A vastly experienced boss who had led England at major tournaments.

Crystal Palace.q: Which French great managed Palace from 2021 to 2023 in his first Premier League job?
Crystal Palace.hint: A World Cup-winning midfielder and former Arsenal captain.

Fulham.q: Craven Cottage sits on the bank of which river?
Fulham.hint: The ground overlooks the river that winds through London.

Fulham.q: What is Fulham's traditional nickname?
Fulham.hint: Taken from the small pavilion at the corner of their ground.

Fulham.q: Which club did Fulham face in the 2010 UEFA Europa League final?
Fulham.hint: A Madrid club, though not the city's most decorated one.

Fulham.q: Which manager led Fulham on their 2010 European run?
Fulham.hint: The boss of that run later took charge of the England national team.

Fulham.q: In which position did the legendary Johnny Haynes make his name?
Fulham.hint: Nicknamed 'The Maestro' for his visionary passing from midfield.

Fulham.q: Who bought Fulham in 1997, transforming the club's fortunes?
Fulham.hint: The businessman who then owned Harrods department store.

Fulham.q: What colour is Fulham's traditional home shirt?
Fulham.hint: The kit earns them the alternative nickname 'The Whites.'

Fulham.q: Fulham won which trophy in 2002, earning a route into Europe?
Fulham.hint: A summer competition that handed the winners a UEFA Cup berth.

Fulham.q: In which part of London is Fulham based?
Fulham.hint: The club lies in the SW6 district beside the Thames.

Fulham.q: Which Italian giant did Fulham famously beat in the 2010 Europa League round of 16?
Fulham.hint: Fulham overturned a first-leg deficit to win 4-1 at home against the Turin side.

Fulham.q: Al-Fayed controversially erected a statue of which entertainer at the ground?
Fulham.hint: The owner honoured the late 'King of Pop' with a widely mocked figure.

Fulham.q: In which decade was Fulham founded?
Fulham.hint: The club began in 1879 as a church Sunday-school team.

Fulham.q: Shahid Khan, who bought Fulham in 2013, also owns which NFL franchise?
Fulham.hint: He also owns this Florida-based American football team.

Fulham.q: Which manager won the Second Division title with Fulham in 1998-99?
Fulham.hint: A former England captain who later managed the national team.

Fulham.q: Fulham's all-time leading goalscorer is which Welsh striker?
Fulham.hint: His 159 goals across two spells remain the club record; the others are Welsh strikers who never played there.

Fulham.q: Fulham signed striker Aleksandar Mitrovic from which club?
Fulham.hint: He arrived from the north-east club where he had struggled for game time.

Fulham.q: Which manager guided Fulham to the 2021-22 Championship title?
Fulham.hint: A Portuguese boss who took the second-tier title in his first season in charge.

Fulham.q: Who scored the only goal in Fulham's 2018 playoff final win over Aston Villa?
Fulham.hint: The club captain's first-half strike settled the Wembley final.

Fulham.q: Which World Cup-winning defender wound down his career at Fulham in the mid-1970s?
Fulham.hint: England's 1966 World Cup-winning captain finished up at the Cottage.

Fulham.q: Fulham lost the 1975 FA Cup final to which club?
Fulham.hint: They fell to the East London side, beaten by two Alan Taylor goals.

Fulham.q: Louis Saha left Fulham in January 2004 to join which club?
Fulham.hint: A mid-season move took the French striker to Old Trafford.

Fulham.q: Which two ageing superstars famously joined Fulham in the mid-1970s?
Fulham.hint: A mercurial Northern Irishman and a flamboyant England forward drew the crowds.

Fulham.q: Fulham's highest-ever top-flight finish came in 2008-09 — where did they finish?
Fulham.hint: The placing earned a Europa League spot, their best top-flight finish.

Fulham.q: Which engineer designed Craven Cottage's historic pavilion and main stand?
Fulham.hint: The Scotsman behind many of Britain's early football grandstands.

Fulham.q: During Craven Cottage's early-2000s redevelopment, Fulham groundshared at which stadium?
Fulham.hint: They shared the home of west-London neighbours QPR.

Fulham.q: Joe Bryan scored both Fulham goals in the 2020 playoff final against which club?
Fulham.hint: A west-London rival beaten 2-1 after extra time at Wembley.

Fulham.q: Joao Palhinha, a standout Fulham midfielder signed in 2022, plays for which nation?
Fulham.hint: A defensive midfielder capped by the Selecao das Quinas.

Fulham.q: How many points did Tigana's Fulham amass winning the 2000-01 First Division title?
Fulham.hint: A then-record haul for the division, backed by 90 goals scored.

Fulham.q: How many league goals did Mitrovic score in the record-breaking 2021-22 Championship season?
Fulham.hint: He surpassed Guy Whittingham's long-standing tally of 42.

Fulham.q: Which Fulham teenager became the Premier League's youngest-ever player in May 2019?
Fulham.hint: Aged 16 years and 30 days on his debut, before moving to Liverpool.

Fulham.q: Who became the youngest manager in Premier League history when Fulham appointed him in 2003?
Fulham.hint: A Welshman who turned to management after a car crash ended his playing days.

Fulham.q: On the final day of 2007-08, whose header secured Fulham's 'Great Escape' survival at Portsmouth?
Fulham.hint: The captain's 76th-minute goal at Fratton Park kept them up on goal difference.

Fulham.q: Which former Fulham player, as PFA chairman, campaigned to abolish the maximum wage in 1961?
Fulham.hint: Later a famous broadcaster known for his beard and prominent chin.

Fulham.q: After the wage cap was lifted, which Fulham man became English football's first 100-pounds-a-week player in 1961?
Fulham.hint: The club's iconic playmaker and England captain was the first to benefit.

Fulham.q: Which one-club Fulham right-back won the World Cup with England in 1966?
Fulham.hint: The Fulham full-back is an uncle of a rugby World Cup winner.

Brighton.q: What colours are Brighton's traditional home shirts?
Brighton.hint: The classic vertically striped seaside strip.

Brighton.q: Brighton & Hove Albion are based in which English county?
Brighton.hint: A south-coast city sitting between the sea and the South Downs.

Brighton.q: To which club was Moisés Caicedo sold in 2023?
Brighton.hint: The roughly £115m move set a British transfer record at the time.

Brighton.q: Alexis Mac Allister left Brighton in 2023 to join which club?
Brighton.hint: The Argentina World Cup winner moved to Anfield.

Brighton.q: What nationality is former Brighton head coach Roberto De Zerbi?
Brighton.hint: He had previously managed Sassuolo in his homeland's top flight.

Brighton.q: Graham Potter left Brighton in 2022 to manage which club?
Brighton.hint: He departed for a west London club early in the season.

Brighton.q: Who is Brighton's long-serving club captain, an academy-graduate defender?
Brighton.hint: The centre-back came through the youth ranks and earned an England cap in 2018.

Brighton.q: What nationality is Brighton winger Kaoru Mitoma?
Brighton.hint: He joined from Kawasaki Frontale.

Brighton.q: Which manager led Brighton to promotion to the Premier League in 2017?
Brighton.hint: He guided the club up as Championship runners-up.

Brighton.q: Brighton reached the 1983 FA Cup Final, losing a replay to which club?
Brighton.hint: The first game finished 2-2, remembered for the 'and Smith must score' miss.

Brighton.q: What is Brighton's highest ever league finish?
Brighton.hint: Achieved in 2022-23, it secured European football for the first time.

Brighton.q: Who became Brighton's club-record signing when he joined from Leeds United in 2024?
Brighton.hint: The French forward arrived for a reported £40m fee.

Brighton.q: In which year was Brighton & Hove Albion founded?
Brighton.hint: The club was formed at the turn of the 20th century.

Brighton.q: Which club did Brighton sell Ben White to in 2021?
Brighton.hint: The England defender moved to north London for around £50m.

Brighton.q: Midfielder Yves Bissouma left Brighton in 2022 for which London club?
Brighton.hint: The Mali international swapped the south coast for north London.

Brighton.q: Bobby Zamora's goals drove Brighton to back-to-back promotions in which era?
Brighton.hint: He won successive third- and second-tier titles in 2001 and 2002.

Brighton.q: Which manager won Brighton the League One title in 2010-11?
Brighton.hint: The Uruguayan's side amassed a club-record points tally in their final Withdean season.

Brighton.q: What was Brighton's nickname before 'The Seagulls'?
Brighton.hint: An earlier marine mascot tied to the seaside town.

Brighton.q: What is the approximate capacity of the Amex Stadium?
Brighton.hint: It was expanded from around 22,500 after opening.

Brighton.q: Which national team did Moisés Caicedo represent at the 2022 World Cup?
Brighton.hint: He featured for the side that faced hosts Qatar in the tournament's opening match.

Brighton.q: Brighton reached the Europa League knockout stage in 2023-24, where they were eliminated by which club?
Brighton.hint: José Mourinho's Italian side ended the run in the round of 16.

Brighton.q: Which long-serving Brighton midfielder joined in 2017 and later left for Borussia Dortmund?
Brighton.hint: The German creator was a fixture across the club's early Premier League years, departing in 2024.

Brighton.q: On the final day of 1996-97, Brighton avoided relegation from the Football League with a draw at which club?
Brighton.hint: The 1-1 result instead sent the hosts out of the League.

Brighton.q: After leaving the Goldstone Ground, Brighton groundshared for two seasons at which distant club's stadium?
Brighton.hint: Fans faced a long trek to a Kent ground from 1997.

Brighton.q: Which temporary home did Brighton use from 1999 until 2011?
Brighton.hint: An athletics venue with a running track on the edge of the city.

Brighton.q: Which player scored a spectacular long-range goal for Brighton against Manchester City in 2023?
Brighton.hint: The young Paraguayan's dipping strike earned a 1-1 home draw.

Brighton.q: Which experienced England striker joined Brighton on a free transfer in 2020?
Brighton.hint: A former Manchester United and Arsenal forward became a reliable option up front.

Brighton.q: In which position does Brighton's Bart Verbruggen, signed in 2023, play?
Brighton.hint: The young Dutchman competes to keep goal at the Amex.

Brighton.q: Adam Lallana joined Brighton in 2020 from which club?
Brighton.hint: The England midfielder arrived on a free from the reigning Premier League champions.

Brighton.q: Tony Bloom, Brighton's owner, also owns which Belgian football club?
Brighton.hint: He invested in the Brussels side that rose to challenge for the Belgian title.

Brighton.q: Brighton first reached English football's top division in 1979 under which manager?
Brighton.hint: The former England midfielder led the club into the old First Division.

Brighton.q: In 2022-23 Brighton reached the semi-final of which cup, losing to Manchester United on penalties at Wembley?
Brighton.hint: Solly March's missed spot-kick ended the run in England's oldest knockout competition.

Brighton.q: Which national team did former Brighton goalkeeper Robert Sánchez represent?
Brighton.hint: Though he has an English mother, he opted to play for his father's country in Iberia.

Brighton.q: What is the Amex Stadium's full official name?
Brighton.hint: The name reflects both the credit-card sponsor and its role for the local area.

Brighton.q: Which Brazilian forward did Brighton sign from Watford in 2023 before selling him to Chelsea in 2025?
Brighton.hint: He joined from Vicarage Road and became a key forward before a big-money exit.

Brighton.q: Which pacy defender did Brighton sign from Chelsea's academy in January 2020?
Brighton.hint: The England youth international thrived down Brighton's right flank.

Brighton.q: Who are Brighton's fiercest rivals, contested in the so-called M23 derby?
Brighton.hint: The 'Eagles' rivalry is bound up with the very origin of Brighton's own nickname.

Brighton.q: In which country was Brighton head coach Fabian Hürzeler born?
Brighton.hint: Though he coached in Germany, he holds dual nationality from his birthplace across the Atlantic.
